title: Legal Practice Migration: Secure, Chain-of-Custody Packing for Active Case Archives and High-Value Documents
excerpt: Legal practice migration: chain-of-custody packing active case archives high-value documents law firm office relocation Addis secure move.
content:
Legal practice migration: secure, chain-of-custody packing for active case archives and high-value documents

Law firm move is not office furniture shuffle—active case archives demand chain-of-custody, client confidentiality, and zero misfiled folder that becomes malpractice exposure. Legal practice migration sequences sealed boxes, logged handoffs, and after-hours load preference.

This guide migrates legal practices with secure chain-of-custody for case archives and documents.

Chain-of-custody framework

  • Box numbered seal tamper-evident.
  • Log sheet box ID matter name custodian sign each handoff.
  • Active cases separate inactive archive.
  • Partner escort or trusted clerk truck ride optional tier.
  • Destruction certificates shredded before move if policy.

Packing standards

File cartons upright; no loose stack; climate dry truck; fire-rated safe separate subcrew; laptops encrypted hand-carry.

IT and practice management

Server backup verify; cloud sync before shutdown; phone forwarding day-of; court deadline calendar freeze move week if possible.

Access and timing

After-hours elevator; COI building; confidentiality agreement mover crew sign; no subcontract surprise crew.

Active Movers legal tier

Sealed box protocol; custody log template; senior crew background stable roster.

Conclusion

Legal practice migration succeeds when case archives move sealed logged and escorted—chain-of-custody is professional duty not moving extra.
author: Active Movers Team
